ground_truth.title: Guide to filing your taxes in 2025
ground_truth.main_content: The IRS offers an online tax filing system that lets you file at no cost called Direct File. It is available in over 20 states. The end of the 2025 tax season for most Americans is April 15, 2025. If you are unable to file before that date, you still have options: you can file for an extension, file a late return without an extension, or file as soon as you can to receive your refund. If you have income below the standard deduction threshold for 2024, which is $14,600 for single filers and $29,200 for those married filing jointly, you may not be required to file a return, but you may want to file anyway to claim tax credits. If you worked during 2024 and had taxes withheld from your paycheck, you may be able to get some or all of that over-withholding back in your refund. If you worked or were self-employed and had earned income under $66,819, you could receive the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) by filing a tax return, with maximum amounts ranging from $632 to $7,830 depending on the number of qualifying children. The Child Tax Credit is worth a maximum of $2,000 per qualifying child, with up to $1,700 being refundable. If you are one of the estimated 100 million people that are eligible to file your tax return for free, you can keep all of your refund money by choosing from several options: IRS Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA), AARP Tax-Aide, Tax Counseling for the Elderly (TCE), IRS Free File, or MilTax for service members. Before using a fee-based tax preparer, check their qualifications using the IRS directory, ask about service fees, and ensure they sign the return and include their PTIN. Refund Anticipation Checks (RAC) allow you to pay preparation fees out of your refund instead of upfront, typically costing $30 to $50, while Refund Advance Loans (RAL) let you borrow money upfront against your expected refund. Electronically filing and choosing direct deposit is the fastest way to get your refund, with the IRS normally issuing refunds within 21 days. Scammers usually target you by impersonating the IRS to get you to share your personal information; the IRS does not initiate contact with taxpayers by email, text messages, or social media channels to request personal or financial information.

The end of the 2025 tax season for most Americans is April 15, 2025.
If you worked or were self-employed and had earned income under $66,819, you could receive the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) by filing a tax return.
Electronically filing and choosing direct deposit is the fastest way to get your refund.
The IRS does not initiate contact with taxpayers by email, text messages, or social media channels to request personal or financial information.
without: An official website of the United States government
